goal: Draft a mutual non-disclosure agreement between Acme Corp and Beta LLC, governed by Washington law, for evaluating a potential partnership. Use only the facts given; mark every unknown field as [BLANK]. Include the attorney-review disclaimer.
final_report:
**Attorney-Review Disclaimer (Read This First)**

This draft Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement is a generic template prepared by an AI system based only on the limited facts you provided. It does not account for all laws, regulations, or market-specific practices that may apply to your situation, including but not limited to:

- Market-standard benchmarks for NDAs in your industry or transaction type.
- Jurisdiction-specific rules under Washington law (or any other jurisdiction), including any special requirements for enforceability, remedies, or limitations on confidentiality obligations.
- Any flow-down obligations that may be required if either party is subject to third‑party confidentiality, data protection, government contracting, or similar obligations.
- Any non‑compete, non‑solicitation, or non‑circumvention ("NNN") maintenance matrix or tracking of related obligations over time.
- Any ratification or consent requirements that might apply if this NDA interacts with other agreements (for example, amendments, subleases, assignments, or corporate approvals).
- Any employment, labour, export control, data privacy, or other regulatory requirements (including, by way of example only, ADS fixed defaults or Pakistan labour law, which are not addressed here and may or may not be relevant to you).

This document is **not** legal advice, does **not** create a lawyer‑client relationship, and may be incomplete or inappropriate for your purposes. **You must have a qualified attorney licensed in the relevant jurisdiction(s) review, customise, and approve this agreement before you use it or rely on it.** Do not sign or circulate this draft as-is.

---

# MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of [BLANK] (the "Effective Date"), by and between:

- **Acme Corp**, a [BLANK] corporation with its principal place of business at [BLANK] ("Acme"); and
- **Beta LLC**, a [BLANK] limited liability company with its principal place of business at [BLANK] ("Beta").

Acme and Beta are sometimes referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

## 1. Purpose

The Parties wish to explore and evaluate a potential business partnership between them (the "Purpose"). In connection with the Purpose, each Party may disclose to the other certain confidential, proprietary, or non‑public information. This Agreement sets out the terms and conditions under which such information will be disclosed and protected.

## 2. Definitions

2.1 **"Confidential Information"** means any non‑public information that is disclosed, directly or indirectly, by or on behalf of a Party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other Party (the "Receiving Party"), whether orally or in writing, visually, electronically, or in any other form, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure. Confidential Information includes, without limitation:

- business plans, strategies, forecasts, and roadmaps;
- financial information, budgets, and projections;
- products, services, technologies, inventions, discoveries, know‑how, trade secrets, and intellectual property (whether or not patentable);
- customer, supplier, and partner lists and information;
- pricing, costs, and other commercial terms;
- technical data, specifications, designs, drawings, and documentation; and
- any analyses, compilations, studies, notes, or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives (as defined below) that contain or are derived from any of the foregoing.

2.2 **"Representatives"** means, with respect to a Party, that Party’s and its affiliates’ directors, officers, employees, contractors, professional advisors (including legal, financial, and accounting advisors), and potential financing sources who need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those set out in this Agreement.

## 3. Obligations of Confidentiality

3.1 **Use and Disclosure.** The Receiving Party shall:

(a) use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information solely for the Purpose; and

(b) not disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to any person or entity except to its Representatives who need to know such information for the Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations as described in Section 2.2.

3.2 **Standard of Care.** The Receiving Party shall protect the confidentiality of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, and in no event less than a reasonable degree of care.

3.3 **Responsibility for Representatives.** The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives and shall take all reasonable measures to ensure that its Representatives comply with this Agreement. To the extent the Receiving Party has any flow‑down or pass‑through confidentiality obligations to third parties that are relevant to the Purpose, the Receiving Party is responsible for ensuring that its own separate agreements and internal controls address those obligations; such third‑party obligations are **not** addressed or allocated by this Agreement and must be handled separately with counsel.

## 4. Exclusions

The obligations in Section 3 do not apply to any information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate by written records:

(a) is or becomes generally available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Representatives;

(b) was lawfully in the Receiving Party’s possession without restriction on use or disclosure prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party;

(c) is lawfully disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party without restriction on use or disclosure and without breach of any obligation of confidentiality owed to the Disclosing Party; or

(d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party or its Representatives without use of or reference to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.

## 5. Required Disclosures

If the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives is required by law, regulation, or legal process (including by subpoena, court order, or governmental or regulatory request) to disclose any of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, the Receiving Party may make such disclosure, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) the Receiving Party:

(a) promptly notifies the Disclosing Party in writing of the requirement so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy; and

(b) discloses only that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be disclosed, while using reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the remaining Confidential Information.

## 6. No License; No Obligation to Proceed

6.1 **No License.** All Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. No license or other rights, express or implied, in or to any intellectual property or other rights of the Disclosing Party are granted to the Receiving Party by this Agreement, except for the limited right to use the Confidential Information for the Purpose.

6.2 **No Obligation to Proceed.** Nothing in this Agreement obligates either Party to proceed with any transaction, partnership, or other business relationship. Each Party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time, without liability of any kind, subject only to the obligations expressly set out in this Agreement.

## 7. Term and Survival

7.1 **Term.** This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until the earlier of: (a) [BLANK] years after the Effective Date; or (b) the date on which the Parties enter into a definitive written agreement governing their partnership or other business relationship that expressly supersedes this Agreement with respect to the subject matter hereof (the "Term").

7.2 **Survival.** Notwithstanding the expiration or termination of this Agreement, each Receiving Party’s obligations under Sections 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 11, and 13 shall survive for a period of [BLANK] years after the end of the Term, or for such longer period as may be required under applicable law with respect to trade secrets or similar protected information.

## 8. Return or Destruction of Confidential Information

Upon the earlier of (a) the Disclosing Party’s written request, or (b) the expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall promptly, at the Disclosing Party’s option, either return or destroy all copies of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in its possession or control, in any form or medium. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain:

(a) copies of Confidential Information that are stored in routine backup or archival systems, provided that such copies remain subject to the confidentiality obligations in this Agreement for so long as they are retained; and

(b) one (1) copy of the Confidential Information in its legal files solely for the purpose of monitoring compliance with this Agreement.

## 9. Remedies

The Parties acknowledge that monetary damages may be an inadequate remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that such a breach may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party. Accordingly, in addition to any other rights and remedies available at law or in equity, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief (including temporary, preliminary, and permanent injunctions) and specific performance, without the necessity of posting a bond or proving actual damages, to prevent or curtail any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement.

## 10. No Warranty

ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY, WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING ANY WARRANTIES OF ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, NON‑INFRINGEMENT, MERCHANTABILITY, OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

## 11. No Agency; No Exclusivity

11.1 **Independent Parties.** The Parties are independent contractors. Nothing in this Agreement creates any agency, partnership, joint venture, or other form of joint enterprise, employment, or fiduciary relationship between the Parties.

11.2 **No Exclusivity.** Unless expressly agreed in a separate written agreement signed by both Parties, this Agreement does not create any exclusivity between the Parties. Each Party remains free to evaluate or enter into similar discussions or agreements with third parties, subject to its obligations under this Agreement and any separate agreements.

## 12. Assignment; Successors and Assigns

Neither Party may assign or transfer this Agreement, in whole or in part, whether by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that either Party may assign this Agreement without such consent to a successor in interest in connection with a merger, acquisition, corporate reorganisation, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets or business to which this Agreement relates. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Section 12 is void. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement is binding upon and inures to the benefit of the Parties and their respective successors and permitted assigns.

## 13. Governing Law; Venue

This Agreement and any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby (whether in contract, tort, or otherwise) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Washington, without giving effect to any choice or conflict of law provision or rule that would cause the laws of any other jurisdiction to apply.

The Parties agree that any legal action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in [BLANK], Washington, and each Party irrevocably submits to the personal jurisdiction and venue of such courts and waives any objection based on inconvenient forum or lack of jurisdiction.

## 14. Notices

All notices and other communications under this Agreement must be in writing and will be deemed given when delivered personally, sent by a nationally recognised overnight courier service, or sent by registered or certified mail (return receipt requested, postage prepaid), to the Parties at the following addresses (or to such other address as a Party may designate by written notice to the other Party):

For Acme:

Acme Corp  
Attn: [BLANK]  
Address: [BLANK]  
Email: [BLANK]

For Beta:

Beta LLC  
Attn: [BLANK]  
Address: [BLANK]  
Email: [BLANK]

Notices sent by email alone are not sufficient unless expressly agreed in writing by both Parties.

## 15. Entire Agreement; Amendments; Waivers

15.1 **Entire Agreement.** This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings, agreements, negotiations, representations, and warranties, both written and oral, with respect to such subject matter.

15.2 **Amendments.** This Agreement may be amended, modified, or supplemented only by a written instrument signed by authorised representatives of both Parties. If this Agreement is later incorporated into, amended by, or superseded by another agreement (for example, a definitive partnership agreement), the Parties should ensure that any ratification, consent, or related requirements in such other agreement are addressed separately with counsel; this Agreement does not itself operate as a ratification of any other contract, sublease, or arrangement.

15.3 **Waivers.** No waiver by either Party of any breach or default under this Agreement is effective unless in writing and signed by an authorised representative of the waiving Party. No waiver of any breach or default is a waiver of any other or subsequent breach or default.

## 16. Severability

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect, the remaining provisions will remain in full force and effect, and the invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision will be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable while preserving, to the greatest extent possible, the Parties’ original intent.

## 17. Counterparts; Electronic Signatures

This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which is deemed an original, but all of which together constitute one and the same instrument. Signatures delivered by facsimile, PDF, or other electronic means will be deemed to have the same legal effect as original signatures to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law.

---

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have caused this Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement to be executed by their duly authorised representatives as of the Effective Date.

**ACME CORP**  
By: _______________________________  
Name: [BLANK]  
Title: [BLANK]  
Date: [BLANK]

**BETA LLC**  
By: _______________________________  
Name: [BLANK]  
Title: [BLANK]  
Date: [BLANK]

args.report:
MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of [BLANK] (the "Effective Date"), by and between Acme Corp, a [BLANK] corporation with its principal place of business at [BLANK] ("Acme"), and Beta LLC, a [BLANK] limited liability company with its principal place of business at [BLANK] ("Beta"). Acme and Beta are sometimes referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

RECITALS

A. The Parties wish to explore and evaluate a potential partnership between them (the "Proposed Relationship").

B. In connection with the Proposed Relationship, each Party may disclose to the other certain confidential, proprietary, or non-public information.

C. The Parties desire to protect the confidentiality of such information in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises contained herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows:

1. Definitions

1.1 "Confidential Information" means any non-public, confidential, or proprietary information disclosed by or on behalf of a Party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other Party (the "Receiving Party"), whether disclosed before or after the Effective Date, and whether disclosed orally, visually, in writing, electronically, or in any other form, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure. Confidential Information includes, without limitation: business plans; financial information; pricing; marketing plans; customer and supplier information; product plans; technical data; software (including source code and object code); inventions; know-how; processes; designs; trade secrets; and information relating to the Proposed Relationship.

1.2 "Representatives" means a Party's and its affiliates' directors, officers, employees, contractors, advisors (including legal, financial, and accounting advisors), and other agents who have a need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose (as defined below) and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those set forth in this Agreement.

1.3 "Purpose" means evaluating, discussing, and negotiating the Proposed Relationship between the Parties, and any directly related internal analysis and decision-making.

2. Mutual Confidentiality Obligations

2.1 Use and Disclosure. The Receiving Party shall:
(a) use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information solely for the Purpose; and
(b) not disclose the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to any third party except to its Representatives who have a need to know such information for the Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those in this Agreement.

2.2 Standard of Care. The Receiving Party shall protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care.

2.3 Responsibility for Representatives. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives and shall take reasonable steps to prevent and remedy any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information by its Representatives.

3. Exclusions

Confidential Information does not include information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate by competent written evidence:
(a) is or becomes generally available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Representatives;
(b) was rightfully in the Receiving Party's possession without restriction on use or disclosure prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party;
(c) is rightfully received by the Receiving Party from a third party without restriction on use or disclosure and without breach of any obligation of confidentiality owed to the Disclosing Party; or
(d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of or reference to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information.

4. Compelled Disclosure

4.1 Legal Requirement. If the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives is required by law, regulation, or valid legal process (including subpoena, court order, or governmental demand) to disclose any of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, the Receiving Party may make such disclosure, provided that, to the extent legally permitted, the Receiving Party:
(a) promptly notifies the Disclosing Party in writing of the requirement so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy; and
(b) cooperates reasonably with the Disclosing Party (at the Disclosing Party's expense) in seeking such protective order or other remedy.

4.2 Limited Disclosure. If a protective order or other remedy is not obtained, the Receiving Party may disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information that it is legally required to disclose, and shall use reasonable efforts to ensure that confidential treatment is accorded to the disclosed information.

5. Ownership; No License

5.1 Ownership. All Confidential Information remains the sole and exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. No rights or licenses, express or implied, in or to any Confidential Information or any intellectual property of the Disclosing Party are granted to the Receiving Party under this Agreement, except the limited right to use the Confidential Information for the Purpose in accordance with this Agreement.

5.2 No Obligation to Proceed. Nothing in this Agreement obligates either Party to proceed with any transaction, business relationship, or further agreement, and each Party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to terminate discussions and negotiations regarding the Proposed Relationship at any time.

6. Term and Survival

6.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue in effect until [BLANK] (the "Term"), unless earlier terminated by either Party upon [BLANK] days' prior written notice to the other Party.

6.2 Survival of Obligations. Notwithstanding any expiration or termination of this Agreement, each Receiving Party's obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall continue for a period of [BLANK] years after the date of disclosure of such Confidential Information, or, with respect to trade secrets, for so long as such information remains a trade secret under applicable law.

7. Return or Destruction of Confidential Information

Upon the earlier of (a) the Disclosing Party's written request or (b) termination or expiration of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall promptly, at the Disclosing Party's option, return or destroy all copies of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in its possession or control, in any form or medium, and certify in writing to the Disclosing Party that it has complied with this obligation. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain copies of Confidential Information (i) as required by applicable law or regulation, or (ii) in accordance with its standard archival or backup procedures, provided that any retained Confidential Information shall remain subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement for so long as it is retained.

8. Remedies

8.1 Equitable Relief. The Receiving Party acknowledges that any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party for which monetary damages may be an inadequate remedy. Accordingly, in the event of any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled, in addition to any other rights and remedies available at law or in equity, to seek immediate injunctive or other equitable relief without the necessity of posting bond or proving actual damages.

8.2 Other Remedies. Nothing in this Agreement limits any other remedies that may be available to the Disclosing Party at law or in equity for breach of this Agreement.

9. No Warranty

ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY WARRANTY OF ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR NON-INFRINGEMENT.

10. No Publicity

Neither Party shall issue any press release or make any public announcement regarding the existence or terms of this Agreement or the Proposed Relationship without the prior written consent of the other Party, except as required by applicable law or regulation.

11. Notices

All notices and other communications under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed given when (a) delivered personally, (b) sent by a nationally recognized overnight courier with tracking, (c) sent by registered or certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, or (d) sent by email with confirmation of transmission, in each case to the Parties at the following addresses or email addresses (or to such other address or email address as a Party may designate by written notice to the other Party):

If to Acme:
Name: [BLANK]
Title: [BLANK]
Address: [BLANK]
Email: [BLANK]

If to Beta:
Name: [BLANK]
Title: [BLANK]
Address: [BLANK]
Email: [BLANK]

12. Governing Law; Venue

12.1 Governing Law. This Agreement and any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Washington, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.

12.2 Venue. The Parties agree that any action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in [BLANK], Washington, and each Party hereby irrevocably submits to the personal jurisdiction and venue of such courts and waives any objection thereto, including any objection based on forum non conveniens.

13. Assignment

Neither Party may assign or transfer this Agreement, in whole or in part, whether by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that either Party may assign this Agreement without such consent to a successor in interest in connection with a merger, acquisition, corporate reorganization, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets, provided that such successor agrees in writing to be bound by this Agreement. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Section 13 shall be null and void.

14. Relationship of the Parties

The Parties are independent contractors. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create a partnership, joint venture, agency, or employment relationship between the Parties. Neither Party has any authority to bind the other Party in any manner.

15. Entire Agreement; Amendments; Waiver

15.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous understandings, agreements, negotiations, and communications, whether written or oral, relating to such subject matter.

15.2 Amendments. This Agreement may be amended or modified only by a written instrument signed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties.

15.3 Waiver. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the Party against whom the waiver is sought to be enforced. No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power, or remedy under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise of any such right, power, or remedy preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power, or remedy.

16. Severability

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such provision shall be enforced to the maximum extent permissible, and the remaining provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.

17. Counterparts; Electronic Signatures

This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Signatures delivered by facsimile, PDF, or other electronic means shall be deemed to have the same legal effect as original signatures.

18. Attorney-Review Disclaimer

THIS DOCUMENT HAS BEEN PREPARED BY AN ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE SYSTEM BASED SOLELY ON LIMITED USER-PROVIDED INFORMATION AND STANDARD CONTRACT PROVISIONS. IT DOES NOT CONSTITUTE LEGAL ADVICE, DOES NOT CREATE AN ATTORNEY-CLIENT RELATIONSHIP, AND MAY NOT ADDRESS OR COMPLY WITH ALL LAWS OR CIRCUMSTANCES APPLICABLE TO THE PARTIES OR THE TRANSACTION. EACH PARTY SHOULD CONSULT WITH ITS OWN QUALIFIED ATTORNEY LICENSED IN THE RELEVANT JURISDICTION (INCLUDING THE STATE OF WASHINGTON) TO REVIEW, MODIFY, AND APPROVE THIS AGREEMENT BEFORE SIGNING.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement as of the Effective Date.

ACME CORP

By: ________________________________
Name: [BLANK]
Title: [BLANK]
Date: [BLANK]

BETA LLC

By: ________________________________
Name: [BLANK]
Title: [BLANK]
Date: [BLANK]

6. No Warranties

All Confidential Information is provided "as is" and without any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to its accuracy or completeness. The Disclosing Party shall have no liability to the Receiving Party resulting from the use of the Confidential Information, except to the extent expressly agreed in a separate written agreement between the Parties.

7. Remedies

7.1 Equitable Relief. The Receiving Party acknowledges that any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party for which monetary damages may be an inadequate remedy. Accordingly, in the event of any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled, in addition to any other rights and remedies available at law or in equity, to seek injunctive or other equitable relief without the necessity of posting bond or proving actual damages.

7.2 Other Remedies. Nothing in this Agreement limits any other remedies that may be available to the Disclosing Party at law or in equity for breach of this Agreement.

8. No Indemnity; No Special Construction Rules

8.1 No Indemnity. This Agreement is limited to confidentiality obligations and related remedies. It does not contain, and shall not be construed to create, any indemnity, defense, hold-harmless, or similar risk-shifting obligations between the Parties. Any such obligations, if agreed, must be set forth in a separate, duly executed written agreement.

8.2 No Construction as Construction Contract. The Parties acknowledge and agree that this Agreement is a mutual non-disclosure agreement solely for the exchange and protection of Confidential Information in connection with evaluating a potential partnership. It is not a construction contract, subcontract, or other agreement for construction, improvement, or repair of real property, and therefore is not intended to address or allocate any obligations relating to construction-specific statutory regimes (including, without limitation, any Washington statutes regarding anti-indemnity, retainage, prompt payment, prevailing wage, or flow-down obligations). Any such matters, if applicable to a later business relationship, must be addressed in a separate, duly executed written agreement.

9. Governing Law; Venue

9.1 Governing Law. This Agreement and any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Washington, without giving effect to any choice or conflict of law provision or rule that would cause the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction.

9.2 Venue. The Parties agree that any legal action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in [BLANK], Washington, and each Party hereby submits to the personal jurisdiction and venue of such courts for such purposes.

10. No Real Property or Lease Terms

10.1 No Lease or Real Property Terms. The Parties acknowledge and agree that this Agreement is not a lease, sublease, license, or other agreement granting any interest in or rights to occupy or use real property. Accordingly, this Agreement does not address, and shall not be construed to address, any matters relating to real property or lease terms, including, without limitation, any triple-net (NNN) maintenance obligations, structural repair allocations, operating expenses, or ratification of amendments or subleases. Any such matters, if applicable to a later business relationship, must be addressed in a separate, duly executed written agreement.

11. No Obligation to Disclose; No Agency

11.1 No Obligation to Disclose. Nothing in this Agreement obligates either Party to disclose any particular Confidential Information. Each Party may, in its sole discretion, determine what, if any, Confidential Information to disclose to the other Party.

11.2 No Agency or Partnership. This Agreement does not create any agency, partnership, joint venture, or other form of legal association between the Parties, and neither Party has any authority to bind the other in any respect.

12. Notices

12.1 Notices. All notices and other communications under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed given when (a) delivered personally; (b) sent by a nationally recognized overnight courier (receipt requested); or (c) sent by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, in each case to the addresses set forth below (or to such other address as a Party may designate by notice to the other Party in accordance with this Section 12.1):

If to Acme:
Acme Corp
Attn: [BLANK]
Address: [BLANK]
Email: [BLANK]

If to Beta:
Beta LLC
Attn: [BLANK]
Address: [BLANK]
Email: [BLANK]

13. Entire Agreement; Amendments; Waivers

13.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings, agreements, representations, and warranties, both written and oral, with respect to such subject matter.

13.2 Amendments. This Agreement may be amended or modified only by a written instrument signed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties.

13.3 Waivers. No waiver by either Party of any breach or default hereunder shall be deemed a waiver of any preceding or subsequent breach or default. Any waiver must be in writing and signed by an authorized representative of the waiving Party.

14. Assignment

Neither Party may assign or transfer this Agreement, in whole or in part, whether by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that either Party may assign this Agreement without such consent to a successor in interest in connection with a merger, acquisition, corporate reorganization, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets, provided that such successor agrees in writing to be bound by this Agreement. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Section 14 shall be null and void.

15. Severability

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect under applicable law, such provision shall be enforced to the maximum extent permissible, and the remaining provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.

16. Counterparts; Electronic Signatures

This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Signatures delivered by facsimile, PDF, or other electronic means shall be deemed to have the same legal effect as original signatures.

17. Attorney-Review Disclaimer

THIS AGREEMENT IS A GENERAL FORM OF MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT PROVIDED FOR INFORMATIONAL AND DRAFTING PURPOSES ONLY. IT MAY NOT ADDRESS ALL FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES, OR LEGAL REQUIREMENTS APPLICABLE TO THE PARTIES OR THEIR TRANSACTION, INCLUDING ANY SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS OF WASHINGTON OR OTHER APPLICABLE LAW. THE PARTIES SHOULD NOT RELY ON THIS DOCUMENT AS LEGAL ADVICE. EACH PARTY IS STRONGLY ENCOURAGED TO CONSULT WITH, AND HAVE THIS AGREEMENT REVIEWED BY, ITS OWN INDEPENDENT LEGAL COUNSEL LICENSED IN THE RELEVANT JURISDICTION(S) BEFORE SIGNING.

18. Signatures

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have caused this Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement to be executed by their duly authorized representatives as of the Effective Date.

ACME CORP

By: ________________________________
Name: [BLANK]
Title: [BLANK]
Date: [BLANK]

BETA LLC

Name: [BLANK]
Title: [BLANK]
Date: [BLANK]

**Attorney-Review Disclaimer (Read This First)**

This draft Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement is a generic template prepared by an AI system based only on the limited facts you provided. It does not account for all laws, regulations, or market-specific practices that may apply to your situation, including but not limited to:

- Market-standard benchmarks for NDAs in your industry or transaction type.

- Any flow-down obligations that may be required if either party is subject to third‑party confidentiality, data protection, government contracting, or similar obligations.
- Any non‑compete, non‑solicitation, or non‑circumvention ("NNN") maintenance matrix or tracking of related obligations over time.
- Any ratification or consent requirements that might apply if this NDA interacts with other agreements (for example, amendments, subleases, assignments, or corporate approvals).
- Any employment, labour, export control, data privacy, or other regulatory requirements (including, by way of example only, ADS fixed defaults or Pakistan labour law, which are not addressed here and may or may not be relevant to you).

This document is **not** legal advice, does **not** create a lawyer‑client relationship, and may be incomplete or inappropriate for your purposes. **You must have a qualified attorney licensed in the relevant jurisdiction(s) review, customise, and approve this agreement before you use it or rely on it.** Do not sign or circulate this draft as-is.

# MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of [BLANK] (the "Effective Date"), by and between:

- **Acme Corp**, a [BLANK] corporation with its principal place of business at [BLANK] ("Acme"); and
- **Beta LLC**, a [BLANK] limited liability company with its principal place of business at [BLANK] ("Beta").

Acme and Beta are sometimes referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

## 1. Purpose

The Parties wish to explore and evaluate a potential business partnership between them (the "Purpose"). In connection with the Purpose, each Party may disclose to the other certain confidential, proprietary, or non‑public information. This Agreement sets out the terms and conditions under which such information will be disclosed and protected.

## 2. Definitions

2.1 **"Confidential Information"** means any non‑public information that is disclosed, directly or indirectly, by or on behalf of a Party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other Party (the "Receiving Party"), whether orally or in writing, visually, electronically, or in any other form, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure. Confidential Information includes, without limitation:

- business plans, strategies, forecasts, and roadmaps;
- financial information, budgets, and projections;
- products, services, technologies, inventions, discoveries, know‑how, trade secrets, and intellectual property (whether or not patentable);
- customer, supplier, and partner lists and information;
- pricing, costs, and other commercial terms;
- technical data, specifications, designs, drawings, and documentation; and
- any analyses, compilations, studies, notes, or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives (as defined below) that contain or are derived from any of the foregoing.

2.2 **"Representatives"** means, with respect to a Party, that Party’s and its affiliates’ directors, officers, employees, contractors, professional advisors (including legal, financial, and accounting advisors), and potential financing sources who need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those set out in this Agreement.

## 3. Obligations of Confidentiality

3.1 **Use and Disclosure.** The Receiving Party shall:

(a) use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information solely for the Purpose; and

(b) not disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to any person or entity except to its Representatives who need to know such information for the Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations as described in Section 2.2.

3.2 **Standard of Care.** The Receiving Party shall protect the confidentiality of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, and in no event less than a reasonable degree of care.

3.3 **Responsibility for Representatives.** The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives and shall take all reasonable measures to ensure that its Representatives comply with this Agreement. To the extent the Receiving Party has any flow‑down or pass‑through confidentiality obligations to third parties that are relevant to the Purpose, the Receiving Party is responsible for ensuring that its own separate agreements and internal controls address those obligations; such third‑party obligations are **not** addressed or allocated by this Agreement and must be handled separately with counsel.

## 4. Exclusions

The obligations in Section 3 do not apply to any information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate by written records:

(a) is or becomes generally available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Representatives;

(b) was lawfully in the Receiving Party’s possession without restriction on use or disclosure prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party;

(c) is lawfully disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party without restriction on use or disclosure and without breach of any obligation of confidentiality owed to the Disclosing Party; or

(d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party or its Representatives without use of or reference to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.

## 5. Required Disclosures

If the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives is required by law, regulation, or legal process (including by subpoena, court order, or governmental or regulatory request) to disclose any of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, the Receiving Party may make such disclosure, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) the Receiving Party:

(a) promptly notifies the Disclosing Party in writing of the requirement so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy; and

(b) discloses only that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be disclosed, while using reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the remaining Confidential Information.

## 6. No License; No Obligation to Proceed

6.1 **No License.** All Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. No license or other rights, express or implied, in or to any intellectual property or other rights of the Disclosing Party are granted to the Receiving Party by this Agreement, except for the limited right to use the Confidential Information for the Purpose.

6.2 **No Obligation to Proceed.** Nothing in this Agreement obligates either Party to proceed with any transaction, partnership, or other business relationship. Each Party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time, without liability of any kind, subject only to the obligations expressly set out in this Agreement.

## 7. Term and Survival

7.1 **Term.** This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until the earlier of: (a) [BLANK] years after the Effective Date; or (b) the date on which the Parties enter into a definitive written agreement governing their partnership or other business relationship that expressly supersedes this Agreement with respect to the subject matter hereof (the "Term").

7.2 **Survival.** Notwithstanding the expiration or termination of this Agreement, each Receiving Party’s obligations under Sections 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 11, and 13 shall survive for a period of [BLANK] years after the end of the Term, or for such longer period as may be required under applicable law with respect to trade secrets or similar protected information.

## 8. Return or Destruction of Confidential Information

Upon the earlier of (a) the Disclosing Party’s written request, or (b) the expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall promptly, at the Disclosing Party’s option, either return or destroy all copies of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in its possession or control, in any form or medium. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain:

(a) copies of Confidential Information that are stored in routine backup or archival systems, provided that such copies remain subject to the confidentiality obligations in this Agreement for so long as they are retained; and

(b) one (1) copy of the Confidential Information in its legal files solely for the purpose of monitoring compliance with this Agreement.

## 9. Remedies

The Parties acknowledge that monetary damages may be an inadequate remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that such a breach may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party. Accordingly, in addition to any other rights and remedies available at law or in equity, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief (including temporary, preliminary, and permanent injunctions) and specific performance, without the necessity of posting a bond or proving actual damages, to prevent or curtail any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement.

## 10. No Warranty

ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY, WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING ANY WARRANTIES OF ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, NON‑INFRINGEMENT, MERCHANTABILITY, OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

## 11. No Agency; No Exclusivity

11.1 **Independent Parties.** The Parties are independent contractors. Nothing in this Agreement creates any agency, partnership, joint venture, or other form of joint enterprise, employment, or fiduciary relationship between the Parties.

11.2 **No Exclusivity.** Unless expressly agreed in a separate written agreement signed by both Parties, this Agreement does not create any exclusivity between the Parties. Each Party remains free to evaluate or enter into similar discussions or agreements with third parties, subject to its obligations under this Agreement and any separate agreements.

## 12. Assignment; Successors and Assigns

Neither Party may assign or transfer this Agreement, in whole or in part, whether by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that either Party may assign this Agreement without such consent to a successor in interest in connection with a merger, acquisition, corporate reorganisation, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets or business to which this Agreement relates. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Section 12 is void. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement is binding upon and inures to the benefit of the Parties and their respective successors and permitted assigns.

## 13. Governing Law; Venue

This Agreement and any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby (whether in contract, tort, or otherwise) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Washington, without giving effect to any choice or conflict of law provision or rule that would cause the laws of any other jurisdiction to apply.

The Parties agree that any legal action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in [BLANK], Washington, and each Party irrevocably submits to the personal jurisdiction and venue of such courts and waives any objection based on inconvenient forum or lack of jurisdiction.

## 14. Notices

All notices and other communications under this Agreement must be in writing and will be deemed given when delivered personally, sent by a nationally recognised overnight courier service, or sent by registered or certified mail (return receipt requested, postage prepaid), to the Parties at the following addresses (or to such other address as a Party may designate by written notice to the other Party):

For Acme:

Acme Corp  
Attn: [BLANK]  
Address: [BLANK]  
Email: [BLANK]

For Beta:

Beta LLC  
Attn: [BLANK]  
Address: [BLANK]  
Email: [BLANK]

Notices sent by email alone are not sufficient unless expressly agreed in writing by both Parties.

## 15. Entire Agreement; Amendments; Waivers

15.1 **Entire Agreement.** This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings, agreements, negotiations, representations, and warranties, both written and oral, with respect to such subject matter.

15.2 **Amendments.** This Agreement may be amended, modified, or supplemented only by a written instrument signed by authorised representatives of both Parties. If this Agreement is later incorporated into, amended by, or superseded by another agreement (for example, a definitive partnership agreement), the Parties should ensure that any ratification, consent, or related requirements in such other agreement are addressed separately with counsel; this Agreement does not itself operate as a ratification of any other contract, sublease, or arrangement.

15.3 **Waivers.** No waiver by either Party of any breach or default under this Agreement is effective unless in writing and signed by an authorised representative of the waiving Party. No waiver of any breach or default is a waiver of any other or subsequent breach or default.

## 16. Severability

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect, the remaining provisions will remain in full force and effect, and the invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision will be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable while preserving, to the greatest extent possible, the Parties’ original intent.

## 17. Counterparts; Electronic Signatures

This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which is deemed an original, but all of which together constitute one and the same instrument. Signatures delivered by facsimile, PDF, or other electronic means will be deemed to have the same legal effect as original signatures to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law.

---

Working outline for Mutual NDA (Acme Corp / Beta LLC, WA law):

1. Title
   - MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

2. Intro / Parties / Effective Date
   - This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of [Effective Date: [BLANK]] by and between Acme Corp, a [jurisdiction: [BLANK]] corporation with its principal place of business at [address: [BLANK]] ("Acme"), and Beta LLC, a [jurisdiction: [BLANK]] limited liability company with its principal place of business at [address: [BLANK]] ("Beta").
   - Each a "Party" and together the "Parties".

3. Purpose
   - Parties wish to exchange certain confidential information for the sole purpose of evaluating a potential partnership between them (the "Purpose").

4. Definitions
   - "Confidential Information" definition (broad, includes written, oral, electronic; marked or understood as confidential; includes business, technical, financial, etc.).
   - Exclusions: (a) already known; (b) publicly available; (c) received from third party without breach; (d) independently developed; (e) disclosed with prior written consent; (f) compelled disclosure with notice.

5. Obligations of Confidentiality
   - Use only for the Purpose.
   - Protect with at least reasonable care / same as own.
   - Limit disclosure to employees, officers, directors, contractors, advisors with need to know and subject to confidentiality obligations.
   - No reverse engineering / no circumventing.

6. Term and Survival
   - Agreement term: from Effective Date until [term: e.g., [BLANK] years] after Effective Date or completion of discussions, whichever is earlier/later (to be specified as [BLANK]).
   - Confidentiality obligations survive for [survival period: [BLANK] years] after termination/expiration.

7. No License; No Obligation to Proceed
   - No license or transfer of IP rights by implication or otherwise.
   - No obligation to proceed with any transaction or partnership; either Party may terminate discussions at any time.

8. Ownership; Return or Destruction
   - Disclosing Party retains all rights in its Confidential Information.
   - Upon written request, Receiving Party to return or destroy Confidential Information, subject to standard archival/back-up exceptions.

9. Compelled Disclosure
   - If legally required to disclose, Receiving Party may do so but must (where lawful) give prompt notice and cooperate reasonably with efforts to seek protective order.

10. Remedies
   - Acknowledgment that unauthorized disclosure/use may cause irreparable harm.
   - Entitlement to seek injunctive relief and other equitable remedies, in addition to any other rights and remedies at law or in equity.

11. Disclaimers
   - Confidential Information provided "as is" without warranties.

12. Governing Law; Venue
   - This Agreement governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Washington, without regard to conflict of laws principles.
   - Optional: venue / jurisdiction in state and federal courts located in [county: [BLANK]], Washington.

13. Miscellaneous / Boilerplate
   - Entire agreement; supersedes prior understandings re subject matter.
   - Amendments in writing signed by both Parties.
   - Assignment restrictions: neither Party may assign without prior written consent, except to successor in interest in merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all assets, with notice.
   - Severability.
   - Waiver.
   - Counterparts; electronic signatures.
   - Notices: to specified addresses / emails [BLANK].

14. Signatures

       Title: [BLANK]
       Date: [BLANK]

       Date: [BLANK]

15. Attorney-Review Disclaimer (for user-facing draft, not in contract text)
   - Clear statement that this is a general template, not legal advice; must be reviewed and customized by a qualified attorney licensed in Washington before use.

MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of [BLANK] (the "Effective Date"), by and between Acme Corp, a [BLANK] corporation with its principal place of business at [BLANK] ("Acme"), and Beta LLC, a [BLANK] limited liability company with its principal place of business at [BLANK] ("Beta"). Acme and Beta may be referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

RECITALS

A. The Parties wish to explore and evaluate a potential partnership between them (the "Purpose").

B. In connection with the Purpose, each Party may disclose to the other certain confidential, proprietary, or non-public information.

C. The Parties desire to protect the confidentiality of such information in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises contained herein, the Parties agree as follows:

1. Definitions.

1.1 "Confidential Information" means any non-public, proprietary, or confidential information disclosed by or on behalf of a Party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other Party (the "Receiving Party"), whether disclosed orally, visually, in writing, electronically, or in any other form, that is (a) designated as confidential at the time of disclosure, or (b) should reasonably be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure. Confidential Information includes, without limitation, information relating to business plans, strategies, products, services, technologies, software, source code, trade secrets, know-how, designs, specifications, financial information, customer and supplier information, pricing, marketing plans, and any analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party that contain or reflect such information.

1.2 "Representatives" means a Party's and its affiliates' directors, officers, employees, contractors, advisors (including legal, financial, and technical advisors), and potential financing sources who have a need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those set forth in this Agreement.

2. Confidentiality Obligations.

2.1 Use and Disclosure. The Receiving Party shall (a) use the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information solely for the Purpose; (b) not disclose the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to any third party except to its Representatives who have a need to know such information for the Purpose; and (c) protect the confidentiality of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, but in no event less than reasonable care.

2.2 Responsibility for Representatives. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives and shall take reasonable measures to ensure that its Representatives comply with the terms of this Agreement.

3. Exclusions.

Confidential Information does not include information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate by competent written evidence: (a) is or becomes generally available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Representatives; (b) was rightfully known to the Receiving Party without restriction on use or disclosure prior to disclosure by the Disclosing Party; (c) is rightfully received by the Receiving Party from a third party without restriction on use or disclosure and without breach of any obligation of confidentiality; or (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of or reference to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information.

4. Compelled Disclosure.

If the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives is required by law, regulation, or legal process (including by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand, or similar process) to disclose any of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, the Receiving Party shall, to the extent legally permitted, promptly notify the Disclosing Party in writing so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, the Receiving Party may disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information that it is legally required to disclose, provided that the Receiving Party uses reasonable efforts to obtain confidential treatment for such disclosed information.

5. Term and Survival.

5.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue in effect until [BLANK], unless earlier terminated by either Party upon [BLANK] days' prior written notice to the other Party.

5.2 Survival. Notwithstanding any termination or expiration of this Agreement, each Receiving Party's obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed hereunder shall continue for a period of [BLANK] years from the date of disclosure of such Confidential Information; provided, however, that with respect to any Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law, the Receiving Party's obligations shall continue for so long as such Confidential Information remains a trade secret under applicable law.

6. No License; No Obligation.

6.1 No License. All Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. Nothing in this Agreement grants the Receiving Party any rights, by license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, except for the limited right to use such Confidential Information for the Purpose in accordance with this Agreement.

6.2 No Obligation. Nothing in this Agreement obligates either Party to proceed with any transaction, relationship, or partnership, or to enter into any further agreement. Each Party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time without liability to the other Party.

7. Return or Destruction.

Upon the earlier of (a) the Disclosing Party's written request, or (b) termination or expiration of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall promptly (i) return to the Disclosing Party or (ii) destroy (and certify such destruction in writing upon request) all copies of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information in its possession or control, in any form or medium, except that the Receiving Party may retain (A) copies as required to comply with applicable law or regulation, and (B) copies maintained in routine backup or archival systems, provided that any retained Confidential Information remains subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement for so long as it is retained.

8. Remedies.

The Receiving Party acknowledges that any breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party for which monetary damages may be an inadequate remedy. Accordingly, in the event of any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled, in addition to any other rights and remedies available at law or in equity, to seek injunctive or other equitable relief without the necessity of posting bond or proving actual damages.

9. No Warranty.

ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY WARRANTY OF ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR NON-INFRINGEMENT.

10. Governing Law; Venue.

This Agreement and any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the Parties' discussions regarding the Purpose shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Washington, without regard to its conflict of laws principles. The Parties agree that the state and federal courts located in [BLANK], Washington shall have exclusive jurisdiction and ve

- Any flow-down obligations that may be required if either party is subject to third‑party confidentiality, data protection, government contracting, or similar obligations.
- Any non‑compete, non‑solicitation, or non‑circumvention ("NNN") maintenance matrix or tracking of related obligations over time.
- Any ratification or consent requirements that might apply if this NDA interacts with other agreements (for example, amendments, subleases, assignments, or corporate approvals).
- Any employment, labour, export control, data privacy, or other regulatory requirements (including, by way of example only, ADS fixed defaults or Pakistan labour law, which are not addressed here and may or may not be relevant to you).

This document is **not** legal advice, does **not** create a lawyer‑client relationship, and may be incomplete or inappropriate for your purposes. **You must have a qualified attorney licensed in the relevant jurisdiction(s) review, customise, and approve this agreement before you use it or rely on it.** Do not sign or circulate this draft as-is.

# MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of [BLANK] (the "Effective Date"), by and between:

- **Acme Corp**, a [BLANK] corporation with its principal place of business at [BLANK] ("Acme"); and
- **Beta LLC**, a [BLANK] limited liability company with its principal place of business at [BLANK] ("Beta").

Acme and Beta are sometimes referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

## 1. Purpose

The Parties wish to explore and evaluate a potential business partnership between them (the "Purpose"). In connection with the Purpose, each Party may disclose to the other certain confidential, proprietary, or non‑public information. This Agreement sets out the terms and conditions under which such information will be disclosed and protected.

## 2. Definitions

2.1 **"Confidential Information"** means any non‑public information that is disclosed, directly or indirectly, by or on behalf of a Party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other Party (the "Receiving Party"), whether orally or in writing, visually, electronically, or in any other form, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure. Confidential Information includes, without limitation:

- business plans, strategies, forecasts, and roadmaps;
- financial information, budgets, and projections;
- products, services, technologies, inventions, discoveries, know‑how, trade secrets, and intellectual property (whether or not patentable);
- customer, supplier, and partner lists and information;
- pricing, costs, and other commercial terms;
- technical data, specifications, designs, drawings, and documentation; and
- any analyses, compilations, studies, notes, or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives (as defined below) that contain or are derived from any of the foregoing.

2.2 **"Representatives"** means, with respect to a Party, that Party’s and its affiliates’ directors, officers, employees, contractors, professional advisors (including legal, financial, and accounting advisors), and potential financing sources who need to know the Confidential Information for the Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those set out in this Agreement.

## 3. Obligations of Confidentiality

3.1 **Use and Disclosure.** The Receiving Party shall:

(a) use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information solely for the Purpose; and

(b) not disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to any person or entity except to its Representatives who need to know such information for the Purpose and who are bound by confidentiality obligations as described in Section 2.2.

3.2 **Standard of Care.** The Receiving Party shall protect the confidentiality of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, and in no event less than a reasonable degree of care.

3.3 **Responsibility for Representatives.** The Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives and shall take all reasonable measures to ensure that its Representatives comply with this Agreement. To the extent the Receiving Party has any flow‑down or pass‑through confidentiality obligations to third parties that are relevant to the Purpose, the Receiving Party is responsible for ensuring that its own separate agreements and internal controls address those obligations; such third‑party obligations are **not** addressed or allocated by this Agreement and must be handled separately with counsel.

## 4. Exclusions

The obligations in Section 3 do not apply to any information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate by written records:

(a) is or becomes generally available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party or its Representatives;

(b) was lawfully in the Receiving Party’s possession without restriction on use or disclosure prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party;

(c) is lawfully disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party without restriction on use or disclosure and without breach of any obligation of confidentiality owed to the Disclosing Party; or

(d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party or its Representatives without use of or reference to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.

## 5. Required Disclosures

If the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives is required by law, regulation, or legal process (including by subpoena, court order, or governmental or regulatory request) to disclose any of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, the Receiving Party may make such disclosure, provided that (to the extent legally permitted) the Receiving Party:

(a) promptly notifies the Disclosing Party in writing of the requirement so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy; and

(b) discloses only that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be disclosed, while using reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the remaining Confidential Information.

## 6. No License; No Obligation to Proceed

6.1 **No License.** All Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. No license or other rights, express or implied, in or to any intellectual property or other rights of the Disclosing Party are granted to the Receiving Party by this Agreement, except for the limited right to use the Confidential Information for the Purpose.

6.2 **No Obligation to Proceed.** Nothing in this Agreement obligates either Party to proceed with any transaction, partnership, or other business relationship. Each Party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time, without liability of any kind, subject only to the obligations expressly set out in this Agreement.

## 7. Term and Survival

7.1 **Term.** This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until the earlier of: (a) [BLANK] years after the Effective Date; or (b) the date on which the Parties enter into a definitive written agreement governing their partnership or other business relationship that expressly supersedes this Agreement with respect to the subject matter hereof (the "Term").

7.2 **Survival.** Notwithstanding the expiration or termination of this Agreement, each Receiving Party’s obligations under Sections 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 11, and 13 shall survive for a period of [BLANK] years after the end of the Term, or for such longer period as may be required under applicable law with respect to trade secrets or similar protected information.

## 8. Return or Destruction of Confidential Information

Upon the earlier of (a) the Disclosing Party’s written request, or (b) the expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall promptly, at the Disclosing Party’s option, either return or destroy all copies of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in its possession or control, in any form or medium. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party may retain:

(a) copies of Confidential Information that are stored in routine backup or archival systems, provided that such copies remain subject to the confidentiality obligations in this Agreement for so long as they are retained; and

(b) one (1) copy of the Confidential Information in its legal files solely for the purpose of monitoring compliance with this Agreement.

## 9. Remedies

The Parties acknowledge that monetary damages may be an inadequate remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that such a breach may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party. Accordingly, in addition to any other rights and remedies available at law or in equity, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief (including temporary, preliminary, and permanent injunctions) and specific performance, without the necessity of posting a bond or proving actual damages, to prevent or curtail any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement.

## 10. No Warranty

ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY, WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING ANY WARRANTIES OF ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, NON‑INFRINGEMENT, MERCHANTABILITY, OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

## 11. No Agency; No Exclusivity

11.1 **Independent Parties.** The Parties are independent contractors. Nothing in this Agreement creates any agency, partnership, joint venture, or other form of joint enterprise, employment, or fiduciary relationship between the Parties.

11.2 **No Exclusivity.** Unless expressly agreed in a separate written agreement signed by both Parties, this Agreement does not create any exclusivity between the Parties. Each Party remains free to evaluate or enter into similar discussions or agreements with third parties, subject to its obligations under this Agreement and any separate agreements.

## 12. Assignment; Successors and Assigns

Neither Party may assign or transfer this Agreement, in whole or in part, whether by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that either Party may assign this Agreement without such consent to a successor in interest in connection with a merger, acquisition, corporate reorganisation, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets or business to which this Agreement relates. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Section 12 is void. Subject to the foregoing, this Agreement is binding upon and inures to the benefit of the Parties and their respective successors and permitted assigns.

## 13. Governing Law; Venue

This Agreement and any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby (whether in contract, tort, or otherwise) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Washington, without giving effect to any choice or conflict of law provision or rule that would cause the laws of any other jurisdiction to apply.

The Parties agree that any legal action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in [BLANK], Washington, and each Party irrevocably submits to the personal jurisdiction and venue of such courts and waives any objection based on inconvenient forum or lack of jurisdiction.

## 14. Notices

All notices and other communications under this Agreement must be in writing and will be deemed given when delivered personally, sent by a nationally recognised overnight courier service, or sent by registered or certified mail (return receipt requested, postage prepaid), to the Parties at the following addresses (or to such other address as a Party may designate by written notice to the other Party):

For Acme:

Attn: [BLANK]

Attn: [BLANK]

Notices sent by email alone are not sufficient unless expressly agreed in writing by both Parties.

## 15. Entire Agreement; Amendments; Waivers

15.1 **Entire Agreement.** This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings, agreements, negotiations, representations, and warranties, both written and oral, with respect to such subject matter.

15.2 **Amendments.** This Agreement may be amended, modified, or supplemented only by a written instrument signed by authorised representatives of both Parties. If this Agreement is later incorporated into, amended by, or superseded by another agreement (for example, a definitive partnership agreement), the Parties should ensure that any ratification, consent, or related requirements in such other agreement are addressed separately with counsel; this Agreement does not itself operate as a ratification of any other contract, sublease, or arrangement.

15.3 **Waivers.** No waiver by either Party of any breach or default under this Agreement is effective unless in writing and signed by an authorised representative of the waiving Party. No waiver of any breach or default is a waiver of any other or subsequent breach or default.

## 16. Severability

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect, the remaining provisions will remain in full force and effect, and the invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision will be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable while preserving, to the greatest extent possible, the Parties’ original intent.

## 17. Counterparts; Electronic Signatures

This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which is deemed an original, but all of which together constitute one and the same instrument. Signatures delivered by facsimile, PDF, or other electronic means will be deemed to have the same legal effect as original signatures to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law.
